pattern: A monotonic stack over the second array precomputes each value's next greater neighbour, then a hash lookup answers every query from the subset array.
hints: First solve the next-greater question for the whole second array, then just look up the answers.
A decreasing stack lets you resolve the next greater element for a value the moment a larger one appears.
Pop the stack while the current number exceeds the top, recording the current number as that popped value's next greater; store results in a map keyed by value, then map the first array through it.

edge_inputs.description: largest element has no next greater
strictly decreasing superset all minus one

stress.description: random distinct values with subset drawn from superset
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1000, size))
    nums2 = rng.sample(range(0, 10001), n)
    sub_size = rng.randint(1, n)
    nums1 = rng.sample(nums2, sub_size)
    return [nums1, nums2]
